data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f53740df">
    <title>doc prose: detect definition-by-negation as a structural rule</title>
    <description>
ddx doc prose today catches surface hedge words via contains_any matchers ("robust", "comprehensive", "seamless"). It does not catch a more damaging structural defect: defining a subject by what it isn't.

A real example from a recent HELIX prose pass:

> HELIX models software development as a continuous control loop with seven activities, **not a sequential pipeline**.

The sentence tells the reader what HELIX *is not* (a pipeline) without ever giving the positive shape. The same offense appeared fourteen times across seven documents in different surface forms ("cyclic, not linear", "loop, not pipeline", "not sequential", "not waterfall"). Human review caught it; the prose checker passed it clean.

The defect is structural: a clause whose main predicate is `not <noun-phrase>` with no positive predicate in the same sentence describing what the subject *is*. Prose like that reads as hedging and forces the reader to infer what the author meant.

Proposed rule shape:

Add a new kind: negation_predicate to the ruleSpec schema (cli/internal/docprose/assets.go). Implementation in the embedded checker (cli/internal/docprose/checker.go) scans sentences for the pattern:

  <subject> [is|are|stays|remains] not <noun-phrase|adjective>

Fires only when no positive predicate appears in the same sentence describing the subject. A positive predicate is anything matching `<subject> [is|are|...] <noun-phrase|adjective>` *without* the `not`.

Default rule in the planning pack:

  - id: prose.definition_by_negation
    severity: warning
    kind: negation_predicate
    rationale: This sentence defines its subject by what it is not. Readers infer what you mean; stronger prose names the positive shape directly.
    suggested_edit: Replace the negation with a sentence whose main predicate describes what the subject is. Keep the negation only if a positive predicate follows in the same paragraph.

Scope notes — legitimate uses of negation should not fire:
- Sections explicitly enumerating what something is not ("What HELIX Is Not", "Non-Goals", "Out of Scope", "Anti-Patterns").
- Contrastive constructions where the positive follows immediately ("HELIX is not a CLI; it is a methodology") — the second clause supplies the positive predicate.

The rule needs section-context awareness: ignore matches inside H2/H3 sections whose heading matches `What .+ Is Not|Non-?Goals?|Out of Scope|Anti-Patterns?`. This is the first prose rule that needs heading context, so it likely requires a small extension to the checker per-rule context model.

Out of scope:
- AI-grade semantic detection. The heuristic above catches the structural defect; deeper analysis is a separate request.
- Cross-paragraph context. Start with same-sentence detection; widen if false-negative rate is high in practice.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
New rule prose.definition_by_negation ships in library/checks/prose-quality/rules/planning.yaml.

Test fixture in cli/internal/docprose/testdata catches the rule firing on the bad sentence and NOT firing on the good one:
  - "HELIX is a control loop, not a pipeline." → fires
  - "HELIX is a methodology with seven activities; it is not a pipeline." → does NOT fire (positive predicate present in same sentence)
  - Inside a "## What HELIX Is Not" section: "HELIX is not a CLI." → does NOT fire (section-context suppression)

cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go includes a unit test for the rule covering positive-predicate detection and section-context suppression.

Rule appears in the planning rule pack. Inclusion in technical/public packs is a separate decision in the same change or a follow-up.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>ddx, area:docprose, kind:implementation, prose-quality</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="5545b7edbe749b378be904f2f645d991e032ffc7" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T084548-c8deb96f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the negation-predicate rule in the planning prose checker and pinned it with tests.

- Added `negation_predicate` handling plus section-heading suppression in [cli/internal/docprose/checker.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/cli/internal/docprose/checker.go#L11) and [cli/internal/docprose/checker.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/cli/internal/docprose/checker.go#L103).
- Added the planning rule entry in [library/checks/prose-quality/rules/planning.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/library/checks/prose-quality/rules/planning.yaml#L17).
- Added a focused unit test in [cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go#L253) and a golden fixture under [cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/planning/definition-by-negation/input.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/planning/definition-by-negation/input.md#L1) and [cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/planning/definition-by-negation/findings.golden.json](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f53740df-20260512T084548-c8deb96f/cli/internal/docprose/testdata/fixtures/planning/definition-by-negation/findings.golden.json#L1).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/docprose/...`
- `go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestDocProseCommand.*|TestProseQuality.*'`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit: `967a74cec` `feat: detect definition by negation [ddx-f53740df]`
